paper_title: Cell measurements and re-evaluation of symbiont location

methods.description: This method involves observing and confirming the location of symbiotic R. intracellularis within the host H. hauckii using confocal microscopy and other imaging techniques. The purpose is to provide 3-D evidence of the symbiont's cytosolic location, which was previously only observed in 2-D.

steps.description: Collect wild populations from diverse habitats to study DDA morphological diversity and make cell measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: collect wild populations for morphological study

steps.description: Correlate symbiont trichome length with host diatom cell diameter.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation between trichome length and cell diameter

steps.description: Perform confocal microscopy to observe symbiotic R. intracellularis in H. hauckii.
steps.searchable_summary: use confocal microscopy to observe symbiont location

steps.description: Analyze orthogonal observations (x/y, x/z, y/z projections) to confirm symbiont location.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze 3-D projections to confirm symbiont location

steps.description: Compare observations in chemically fixed field samples to untreated enrichment culture.
steps.searchable_summary: validate symbiont location consistency between fixed and untreated samples

steps.description: Use TEM coupled with immunocytochemistry to show R. intracellularis filaments in host cytosol.
steps.searchable_summary: use TEM and immunocytochemistry to analyze symbiont filaments

steps.description: Perform DAPI staining to show symbiont location near host nucleus.
steps.searchable_summary: use DAPI staining to locate symbiont near nucleus
